name: ReAct
hypothesis: ReAct improves final output by interleaving reasoning steps with tool actions and observations, enabling iterative source lookup, document navigation, verification, and recovery from weak evidence.
baseline: direct generation or single-shot tool call without interleaved reasoning/action loop
benchmark: agent trajectory eval + groundedness/claim verification eval
required_configuration: tool registry; tool schemas; observation schema; policy guardrails; audit logging
expected_runtime_use: agentic research runtime for retrieval, document navigation, verification, and calculation tools
cost_to_measure: local compute/token cost only; no paid SaaS required
latency_to_measure: not blocking per project policy; measure for observability only
risks_to_measure: tool misuse; hallucination propagation; retrieval misalignment; unsupported claims
evidence_generated: agent trajectory traces; tool-call audit ledger; claim-evidence matrix; final answer eval
promotion_criteria: kept as canonical agent pattern; runtime use allowed when tool registry and guardrails exist
rejection_criteria: do not use for tasks that require no tools or where fixed workflow is safer
removal_criteria: remove only if replaced by a strictly more explicit reasoning-action agent policy while preserving equivalent capabilities
substitute_candidate: plan-and-execute; tool-calling agent; hierarchical retrieval interface
substitute_reason: Related but not equivalent; ReAct specifically interleaves reasoning, actions, and observations.
final_filter_reason: kept: foundational free technique that directly improves agentic retrieval/tool-use output quality
allowed_runtime_mode: allowed: prompting/control pattern implemented locally with any free/self-hosted model and local tools
risk_coverage_tags: hallucination;tool misuse;unsupported claims;retrieval poisoning;source conflict;memory poisoning
output_improvement_axes: groundedness;completude;decisão final;cobertura de fontes;auditabilidade;redução de falha silenciosa
free_self_hosted_verification: PASS_TECHNIQUE_ONLY; no license constraint for prompting pattern; implementation must use free/self-hosted model/tools
paid_dependency_risk: none intrinsic; risk only if implemented through paid SaaS agent platform
external_dependency: external tools optional; can be local/self-hosted
mutual_exclusivity_reason: Alternative or complementary to plan-and-execute and tool-calling agents; not equivalent to React frontend framework.
complementarity_policy: retain_complementary_variants; same_family_does_not_mean_duplicate; ReAct complements retrieval planners, document-navigation agents, tool-calling, verification, and plan-and-execute
complementarity_family_peers: plan-and-execute; tool-calling agent; hierarchical retrieval interface; agentic document navigation; self-reflection; critic agent; planner-executor-verifier loop
retention_reason: Foundational agent pattern: interleaves reasoning, tool actions, and observations; fixes missing basic technique and separates it from React frontend framework.
maximal_addition_reason: Added after QA identified missing/falsely merged foundational ReAct agent technique.
